name: Guanajuato (Mexico)

colors.symbolism: the field and details of the arms
gold and the mineral wealth of the state

description: A white field with the coat of arms of the state of Guanajuato centred.
history: Guanajuato has no officially adopted state flag; the design shown — a white field bearing the state coat of arms — is in de facto use to represent the state. The arms themselves were granted to the city by Philip V of Spain in 1741.

features.symbolism: A white field charged with the state coat of arms, in the typical pattern of Mexican state flags.

The arms are based on those granted to the city of Guanajuato by Philip V in 1741, depicting Saint Faith of Conques (Santa Fe) crowned and seated within an oval shield framed by columns and a crown — recalling the city's full original name, Real de Minas de Santa Fe y Real de Minas de Guanajuato, and the silver-mining wealth that made the city one of the richest in colonial New Spain.
